title: NOC 31121 – Dietitians and Nutritionists | GTR Immigration
description: Dietitians and Nutritionists play a critical role in preventive healthcare, chronic disease management, and public health promotion across Canada. With rising awareness of lifestyle-related conditions such as diabetes, obesity, and cardiovascular disease, demand for qualified nutrition professionals continues to grow nationwide. Classified under NOC 31121 (TEER 1), this occupation is eligible under:
duties: Develop, implement, and evaluate nutrition programs
Assess nutritional status and design therapeutic diets
Manage food service systems in hospitals and institutions
Provide nutrition counseling to individuals and groups
Develop educational materials and public health campaigns
Conduct research and analyze nutritional studies
Work in product development within the food industry
Collaborate with healthcare professionals and government agencies
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in dietetics, nutrition, or related field
Graduation from a program accredited by the Partnership for Dietetic Education and Practice (PDEP)
Successful completion of the Canadian Dietetic Registration Exam (CDRE) (required in all provinces except Quebec)
Registration with a provincial regulatory body is mandatory
In other provinces, similar education and training may be required
Membership with Dietitians of Canada is voluntary but recommended for professional development.
job_outlook: While supply and demand are relatively balanced nationally, opportunities are stronger in:

pathways: Growing public health and wellness sector

additional_information: Dietitians and Nutritionists are employed in:
HospitalsLong-term care facilitiesCommunity health centersHome healthcare agenciesEducational institutionsGovernment health departmentsFood & pharmaceutical industriesSports organizations
Typical annual earnings: $42,000–$88,500 CAD, depending on province, specialization, and sector.
Public health initiatives and aging populations continue to support steady demand, particularly in community-based healthcare services.
